data.description:
## Why
The suite grew via characterizeâ†’portâ†’parity during the migration and likely carries change-detector tests (tautological, assertion-light, or pinned-to-implementation rather than behavior) that resist refactoring without catching bugs.

## What
- Stand up mutation testing (e.g. mutmut or cosmic-ray) over src/rebar core modules; record baseline mutation score.
- Identify surviving mutants (untested behavior) AND change-detector tests (tests that pass under mutation / assert nothing meaningful / restate the implementation).
- Prune or rewrite change-detector tests to assert observable behavior; add tests to kill high-value surviving mutants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mutation testing wired (documented invocation) with a recorded baseline score
- [ ] Change-detector tests catalogued and pruned/rewritten to behavioral assertions
- [ ] High-value surviving mutants killed; mutation score improved and recorded
- [ ] Suite still green locally + CI
data.title: Mutation testing: identify and prune change-detector tests
